title: Skill Authoring Rules + Contribute & Edit Workflow
desc: [RULE] ALL skill content (title/desc/steps/tags) MUST be in English -- Korean uses 2-3x tokens. Create, update, and partially edit wkappbot skills. contribute = create or full-update. edit = partial update (title/desc/tags/steps only). Auto version-bumped on every save. When a skill serves both Claude and Codex, include optional operator-friendly flags or notes only when they improve safety, verification, or debugging.
steps: [RULE] Write ALL skill content in English only -- Korean wastes 2-3x tokens and bloats context for every AI reading the skill.
NOTE: | is the step separator in --steps. Do NOT use | inside step text -- it will be split as a new step.
UPDATE (full): wkappbot skill contribute --app X --title ... --desc ... --id existing-id
EDIT title: wkappbot skill edit <id> --title "New Title"
EDIT desc: wkappbot skill edit <id> --desc "New description"
VERIFY refs: wkappbot skill verify <id>  -- checks source_refs still match code
AUDIT all: wkappbot skill audit  -- lists all skills with stale/missing refs
Auto-install: skills/ -> HQ on every publish (skill install runs in build)
[SAFETY] Never directly edit .skill.json with Edit/Write/wkedit tools -- use skill contribute/edit commands only.
[SAFETY] If step content must contain |, rewrite to avoid it (use 'or', 'and', newline description instead).

When documenting v6.0+ multi-pipe architectures, always spell out pipe names explicitly (e.g. wkappbot_eye_ipc vs wkappbot_elevated) plus the must-not-mix rule -- vague descriptions let future sessions conflate separate pipes.

[v6.3+] --add-requirement duplicate guard: adding the same cmd twice to a skill hard-fails immediately (exit 1, no partial save). Check: 'wkappbot skill edit <id> --add-requirement "wkappbot <cmd> => X"' -- if cmd already registered returns [SKILL] FAILED: duplicate requirement cmd. Use --step N --content to UPDATE an existing requirement.

Cross-repo contributor flow: (1) skill read works from any CWD -- all repos share HQ. (2) skill edit auto-forks HQ-only skills into caller's project skills/ (copy-on-edit). (3) Eye syncs peer repo skills/ -> HQ every 30 min; version field resolves conflicts (split-brain detected + warned). (4) suggest add-requirement adds req to suggest + optional --skill updates skill immediately.
author: claude
